name: Wide Ensemble
explanation: A lush bucket-brigade (BBD) stereo chorus ensemble with a vibrato mode. A mono-summed input is fanned into a wide stereo image by two modulated delay lines whose sweep runs in anti-phase on the left and right, so the channels swirl against each other for a big ensemble. The Mode control morphs from chorus (dry plus modulated taps) to vibrato (pure modulated tap, so the whole signal pitch-wobbles). A BBD-darkness Tone low-passes the wet path and a soft saturation emulates compander grit for warm analog character. Controls: Rate, Depth, Mode, Tone and dry/wet Mix.
